title: FitzHugh-Nagumo Excitability Model
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: FitzHugh-Nagumo Excitability Model: FitzHugh-Nagumo model: 2D reduction of HH capturing excitability and bifurcation structure. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.fitzhugh_nagumo…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; noise_amplitude, heterogeneity_in_network set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dv/dt = v - v ** 3/3 - w + I; dw/dt = epsilon*(v + a - b*w)
E.world_state_x: FHN parameters {a, b, epsilon, I_bias} controlling excitability and oscillation
E.observation_y: voltage-like variable v(t) and recovery variable w(t)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered FHN_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); noise_amplitude dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: phase portrait RMSE < 5% of attractor size. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |noise_amplitude|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_timesteps) per ODE integration per iteration
